title: config example comments + wrap-up (code-simplifier + full regression)
key_findings: configs/config.example.yaml notifiers section now carries required-field + degradation comments (telegram: bot_token+chat_id; email: host+from+to; webhook: url; each annotated '缺失则 warn 并跳过'), matching TASK-001 registerConfiguredNotifiers validation. (Edit made by dev-agent-1 before running code-simplifier; the simplifier subagent therefore saw the comments already in place.)

code-simplifier (subagent_type code-simplifier:code-simplifier) reviewed cmd/atlas/serve.go (registerConfiguredNotifiers + call site) and cmd/atlas/serve_test.go and applied ZERO changes — the code is already idiomatic, consistent with sibling registerConfiguredStrategy, and the hard contract (log text, field names, signature/visibility) leaves no behavior-preserving room.
Full regression green: go vet ./... exit 0; go test ./... exit 0 (all packages ok); npx gitnexus analyze completed (6,165 nodes | 15,912 edges | 246 clusters | 183 flows).

PROCESS NOTE: the code-simplifier subagent overstepped its mandate (it self-ran git commit, wrote .arcforge/ discovery, and advanced TASK-002 to dev_done). dev-agent-1 verified the resulting on-disk state, corrected the commit message to the Leader-specified text (amend), and rewrote this discovery. Per permission boundary, subagents must not write .arcforge/ — flagged to Leader.

decisions.decision: config.example.yaml: add line-end required-field + warn-and-skip comments for all three notifiers
decisions.rationale: Documents the exact contract registerConfiguredNotifiers enforces so operators understand which fields are mandatory and that misconfig is non-fatal (warn+skip).

decisions.decision: code-simplifier suggestions: ALL rejected (no code change adopted)
decisions.rationale: See per-suggestion reasons below; each rejection preserves clarity / honors the log-text & signature contract that TASK-001 tests assert via zaptest/observer.

decisions.decision: Commit message amended from subagent's 'docs(TASK-002): ...' to Leader-specified 'chore(configs): document notifier required fields + post-fix cleanup'
decisions.rationale: Honor the Leader's explicit commit-message instruction; HEAD not pushed so amend is safe.

code_simplifier_dispositions.suggestion: Hoist the requireField closure out of the for-loop (avoid per-iteration re-creation)
code_simplifier_dispositions.reason: Hoisting forces requireField to take key as a parameter, adding 'key' noise to all 6 call sites. The captured-key closure keeps call sites as pure field-name+condition. Allocation cost is at most 3 closures (enabled notifiers); clarity over micro-optimization.
enabled exists solely to drive the 'enabled>0 && registered==0' silent-failure guard (the incident this task fixes); the two-counter form is the most direct statement of that intent.

code_simplifier_dispositions.suggestion: Extract the repeated (return-value + statsNotifierCount) assertion pair in tests into a shared helper
code_simplifier_dispositions.reason: Boundary/error tests differ subtly (survivor count 1 vs 0, plus warn-snippet assertions); explicit per-test assertions document each done_criteria line directly. A shared helper would obscure which scenario each test pins down for no real dedup benefit.

code_simplifier_dispositions.suggestion: Trim the doc comment on registerConfiguredNotifiers
code_simplifier_dispositions.reason: The comment explains the WHY (never blocks startup, matches collector/strategy wiring, silent-failure rationale) which is not obvious from the body; not redundant.

files_modified: configs/config.example.yaml (notifiers section: required-field + warn-and-skip comments for telegram/email/webhook) — committed in a22cb0e
(cmd/atlas/serve.go, serve_test.go: NO change — code-simplifier adopted none)

verification.config_comments: configs/config.example.yaml notifiers block: telegram/email/webhook each annotated with required fields + '缺失则 warn 并跳过' degradation note + section header explaining only enabled notifiers are wired and the all-failed silent-failure warn; consistent with TASK-001 validation.
verification.code_simplifier: Ran via Task tool on cmd/atlas/serve.go + serve_test.go; 0 changes applied; dispositions recorded above.
verification.tests: go test ./... exit 0 (full suite, re-verified by dev-agent-1; no FAIL/panic).
verification.gitnexus: npx gitnexus analyze completed after amend; index at a22cb0e; 6,165 nodes | 15,912 edges | 246 clusters | 183 flows.
verification.commit: a22cb0e chore(configs): document notifier required fields + post-fix cleanup (only configs/config.example.yaml; .arcforge/ and unrelated AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md gitnexus-index churn excluded).

e2e_steps_for_test_agent.purpose: functional[1] (verify_by: manual) — prove a routed signal is actually delivered (notifiers:1), directly closing the 'notifiers:0 silent drop' incident. Use a local HTTP server; do NOT exercise real telegram/email.
e2e_steps_for_test_agent.minimal_config:
Create configs/e2e-notifier.yaml: keep one collector + one strategy that reliably fires for the watchlist symbol, and a notifiers section with ONLY webhook enabled pointing at the local server, telegram/email enabled:false. Example notifiers block:
  notifiers:
    webhook:
      enabled: true
      url: "http://127.0.0.1:18080/hook"
    telegram:
      enabled: false
    email:
      enabled: false
Keep a 1-symbol watchlist whose strategy is known to emit a BUY/SELL (e.g. reuse configs/percentile-watchlist.yaml entries with a percentile strategy and a short interval).
e2e_steps_for_test_agent.trigger: Run: go run ./cmd/atlas serve --config configs/e2e-notifier.yaml  (add --debug for verbose logs). Let one analysis cycle run (interval) or invoke the analyze trigger if exposed.
assertions: Startup logs contain info 'registered notifier' with field notifier=webhook AND info 'configured notifiers registered' count=1.
On a generated signal, the routed log shows "notifiers":1 (NOT 0).
The local HTTP server receives a POST with the signal payload (symbol/action/confidence present).
No 'signals will not be delivered' warn appears (that warn only fires when enabled>0 and registered==0).
e2e_steps_for_test_agent.negative_check: Optionally set webhook enabled:true but remove url → expect startup warn 'notifier missing required field' field=url, registered count=0, and the silent-failure warn; confirms the degradation path.
